commands.repair_reason: A contributor needs to add real behavior proof; automation cannot produce evidence from their reported multi-client setup.; Cleared: The diff adds no dependencies, scripts, secret handling, or broader filesystem permissions, and the retry is limited to the existing regular-file identity-mismatch guard. (sha=1417f5d021a658b82aa1c83a8882b7628aa00b39)
commands.review_summary:
The branch tags regular-file identity mismatches as `FsSafeError("path-mismatch")`, retries async JSON read helpers up to three times on that code, and adds changelog plus JSON regression tests.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection and the PR's targeted regression tests: current main throws a raw identity-change error and the async JSON readers do not retry it. I did not run the tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: A contributor needs to add real behavior proof; automation cannot produce evidence from their reported multi-client setup.

target.title: fix(json): retry async JSON reads when atomic rename races
target.body:
## Summary

- Tag the regular-file identity-change throw as `FsSafeError("path-mismatch")` instead of a raw `Error`, matching the sentinel already used in `file-store.ts:149`, `directory-guard.ts:35`, `pinned-write.ts:292`, and `pinned-python.ts:514` for the same temp-file + rename race semantic.
- Wrap `readJson`, `readJsonIfExists`, and `tryReadJson` in a bounded retry (max 3 attempts, 50ms exponential backoff) that only retries on `path-mismatch`, so a concurrent atomic rewrite no longer surfaces as `JsonFileReadError` to callers while parse errors and other read failures still fail fast on the first attempt.
- Add two regression tests in `test/json.test.ts`: one verifying retry-then-success when the file is rotated twice mid-read, one verifying that exceeding the retry budget surfaces `JsonFileReadError { reason: "read" }`.

## Why

`openclaw/openclaw` (and any other consumer of `@openclaw/fs-safe/json`) is hitting `JsonFileReadError: Failed to read JSON file: .../paired.json` under normal multi-client operation. The writer uses temp-file + `rename` (atomic, by design). Concurrent readers race: `statRegularFile` reads the pre-rename inode, `fs.open` then resolves to the post-rename inode, `verifyStableReadTarget` detects the identity mismatch in `src/regular-file.ts:174-179` and throws.

That throw is correct - the read genuinely landed on a different file - but it is a transient state, not corruption. Today the only error type bubbled up is a generic `Error`, so callers cannot retry without doing fragile message matching. The fix typifies it with the existing `path-mismatch` code and adds the bounded retry the issue asks for, scoped to the async public readers.

Tracks openclaw/openclaw#83657.

## Scope notes

- Sync readers (`readJsonSync`, `readRootJsonSync`, `tryReadJsonSync`) are intentionally unchanged - they cannot `await` and the issue's pseudocode is async. A sync retry on top of `Atomics.wait` or a tight spin loop is a separate decision and can land in a follow-up if anyone hits the race on the sync paths.
- `readRegularFile`/`readRegularFileSync` themselves are unchanged in behavior; only the error type the race emits is tightened. Callers that previously string-matched on the message still match the same message.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `readJson(paired.json)` failing with `JsonFileReadError { reason: "read", cause: Error("File changed during read: ...") }` whenever another component performs a temp-file + rename on the same path during the read window.

Real environment tested: focused regression tests in this checkout against the real `readJson`/`readJsonIfExists` paths and the production `readRegularFile` identity check.

Reproduction without the fix (after reverting the two `src/*` edits in this branch, leaving only the new tests in place):

```
$ pnpm test test/json.test.ts
 FAIL  test/json.test.ts > json file helpers > retries readJson when atomic rename races with the read
JsonFileReadError: Failed to read JSON file: ...paired.json
 ❯ readJson src/json.ts:253:11
Caused by: Error: File changed during read: ...paired.json
 ❯ verifyStableReadTarget src/regular-file.ts:178:11
 FAIL  test/json.test.ts > json file helpers > surfaces JsonFileReadError when read races exceed retry budget
AssertionError: expected 1 to be greater than or equal to 3
 Test Files  1 failed (1)
      Tests  2 failed | 12 passed (14)
```

The first failure is the exact bug report. The second failure shows the unfixed code only attempts the read once (no retry budget).

Verification after the fix:

```
$ pnpm test
 Test Files  35 passed (35)
      Tests  383 passed | 2 skipped (385)
$ pnpm build
$ pnpm lint:fs-boundary
$ pnpm lint:file-size
```

All clean.

Observed result after fix: `readJson` survives two back-to-back atomic rename rotations and returns the post-race value; a sustained rotation storm still terminates after the retry budget with the same `JsonFileReadError { reason: "read" }` so corruption and exhaustion remain distinguishable from a successful retry.

What was not tested: a real multi-process Discord + Control UI + CLI reproduction against `openclaw/openclaw`'s gateway, and the sync read paths (out of scope for this PR per the scope note above).

## Verification

- `pnpm test test/json.test.ts`
- `pnpm test`
- `pnpm build`
- `pnpm lint:fs-boundary`
- `pnpm lint:file-size`
target.author: yetval
